content.problemStatement:
Let us solve some more Sorting based problems

Problem statementYou are given an array of NNN integers. Find the minimum number of integers you need to delete from the array such that the absolute difference between each pair of integers in the remaining array will become equal.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum number of integers to be deleted to satisfy the given condition.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases fits in memory limits
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the minimum number of elements that must be deleted so that the absolute difference between every pair of remaining integers is the same.

solution.approach: The condition can be satisfied in two ways. If the common difference D is zero, all remaining numbers must be equal – we can keep all occurrences of the most frequent value. If D>0, any two distinct numbers have difference D, but having a third number would break the condition (either a zero difference with a duplicate or a larger difference). Hence we can keep at most two numbers when D>0. Therefore the optimal size to keep is max(maxFrequency, 2) (except when N=1, we keep the single element). The answer is N minus this size. We count frequencies using a hash map in O(N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) where K is the number of distinct values in the current test case (hash map)
